Module 4:
Your Pre-Prelaunch, Part 5
!
Executive Summary
Continuing...Part 2: Building Early Buzz
Example: JeffWalker.com blog
• 	Always think in terms of sequences to building buzz - this is how you
think in sequences and create attention around your launch
• 	For the blog launch, I didn’t want to do a massive launch, but still wanted
some good buzz
• 	I sent an email to all my lists quickly announcing the new blog and
asking for suggestions on taglines
• 	Offered a new iPad to the winner - on the day the iPad was released!
• 	This creates a positioning where people think about you and your brand
in a very positive way
• 	It’s ultimately an exercise in getting people to think good thoughts about
you
• 	At the end of the survey I also asked “what would you like for me to
cover on my blog in the first 30 days?”
!
1	© 2014 Internet Alchemy, Inc.
GROUPINSIDERS.COM EXCLUSIVE

-- 1 of 2 --

• 	Very important: keep your surveys short
• 	I then came back with another email to the list - reminded about iPad
contest, but also mentioned all the survey responses so far
• 	Insider tip: Never round off numbers - use specificity
When you finally do launch keep the email short and sweet. Use this as an
example of how to build buzz for something that’s not even a product.
!
Key Takeaway
You always can, and should, build buzz around whatever you’re launching.
It’s a powerful strategy that will leave people with a positive impression of
you, and will naturally bring them into your community.
!
